Automation-friendly features? ## Deliverable Structured summary emphasizing multi-repo management.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:18:21.367354634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:51.643571682Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:51.643499298Z","close_reason":"Content for this tool already exists in lib/content.ts with comprehensive whatItDoes, whyItsUseful, implementationHighlights, synergies, techStack, keyFeatures, and useCases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5f3z","title":"Refactor markdown pipeline and optimize flywheel components","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T17:40:07.166801376Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T17:42:48.621735535Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T17:42:48.621735535Z","close_reason":"Optimized TldrToolCard with rAF debouncing for mouse events and simplified mdx.ts path resolution logic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5f3z","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-vz9b","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-15T17:40:07.171619462Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5g7","title":"Design reusable endorsement card component","description":"## Overview Create a flexible, reusable endorsement/testimonial card component that can display quotes from various sources (LinkedIn recommendations, tweets, podcast mentions). ## Component Props ```typescript interface EndorsementCardProps { quote: string; author: string; authorTitle?: string; authorCompany?: string; authorAvatar?: string; source: 'linkedin' | 'twitter' | 'podcast' | 'email' | 'other'; sourceUrl?: string; date?: string; highlight?: boolean; // For featured endorsements } ``` ## Visual Design - Clean card with subtle border/shadow - Source icon badge (LinkedIn blue, X black, etc.) - Author avatar with fallback initials - Quote with proper quotation styling - Optional 'verified' indicator for linked sources ## Variants 1. **Standard**: Full card with all details 2. **Compact**: Just quote and author name 3. **Featured**: Larger, highlighted styling ## Accessibility - Proper citation markup (
, ) - Screen reader friendly - Focus states for interactive elements ## Reusability This component will be used in: - Homepage endorsement showcase - Nvidia story page quote wall - About page testimonials ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All three variants implemented - [ ] Storybook stories for each variant - [ ] TypeScript types exported - [ ] Responsive at all breakpoints - [ ] Accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:07.483439445Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:40:30.373078036Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T21:40:30.373078036Z","close_reason":"Implemented endorsement card component with three variants (standard, compact, featured), five source types (linkedin, twitter, podcast, email, other), spotlight hover effect matching project-card patterns, proper blockquote/cite semantics for accessibility. All static analysis passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} -{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5h5z","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz click should pin a hover-open card instead of closing it","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:02:42.476888446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:02:53.865199156Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5h5z","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:02:42.476888446Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5h5z","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz click should pin a hover-open card instead of closing it","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:02:42.476888446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:03:58.791554212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:03:58.788464715Z","close_reason":"Cross-review fix landed: clicking a hover-open AntiPatternCardsViz card now pins it open; regression test added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5h5z","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:02:42.476888446Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5io","title":"Feature: Mobile bottom sheet for tool details","description":"# Mobile Bottom Sheet for Tool Details ## Overview Replace inline card expansion with a bottom sheet modal on mobile devices. This provides a better mobile UX by not pushing content around and giving more room for details. ## Problem with Current Approach - Expanding a card pushes all content below it down - User loses context of where they were in the grid - Expanded content is cramped in card width - Difficult to compare tools (can only expand one at a time) ## Proposed Solution On mobile (< 768px), clicking "Show More" opens a bottom sheet that: - Slides up from bottom - Contains full tool details - Has close button and swipe-to-dismiss - Leaves card grid visible but dimmed behind ## Design Specifications ### Bottom Sheet Anatomy ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ (Dimmed backdrop) │ │ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ ← Drag handle │ ═══════ │ │ [Icon] Tool Name [✕ Close] │ │ Full tool name • ★ 1.4K │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ What It Does │ │ [Full description text] │ │ │ │ Why It's Useful │ │ [Full explanation] │ │ │ │ Key Features │ │ • Feature 1 │ │ • Feature 2 │ │ │ │ [View on GitHub →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Interaction States - **Opening**: Slides up with spring physics - **Open**: ~80% viewport height, scrollable content - **Dismissing**: Swipe down past threshold or tap backdrop - **Closed**: Slides down, backdrop fades ## Technical Implementation ### Using Framer Motion ```tsx function ToolBottomSheet({ tool, isOpen, onClose }) { return ( {isOpen && ( <> {/* Backdrop */} {/* Sheet */} { if (info.offset.y > 100 || info.velocity.y > 500) { onClose(); } }} className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 max-h-[85vh] rounded-t-3xl bg-slate-900 overflow-hidden" > {/* Drag handle */}
{/* Content */}
{/* Tool details here */}
)} ); } ``` ### Responsive Integration ```tsx // In TldrToolCard const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 767px)'); const [sheetOpen, setSheetOpen] = useState(false); // Different behavior based on viewport const handleExpand = () => { if (isMobile) { setSheetOpen(true); } else { onToggleExpand?.(); // Original inline expansion } }; ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - `components/tool-bottom-sheet.tsx` (new) - Bottom sheet component - `components/tldr-tool-card.tsx` - Conditional expansion behavior - `hooks/use-media-query.ts` (if not exists) - Media query hook ## Accessibility - Focus trap when sheet is open - Escape key closes sheet - `aria-modal="true"` on sheet - Return focus to trigger button on close - Backdrop click closes (with visual indication) ## Performance - Lazy load sheet content (only render when opening) - Use `will-change: transform` for smooth animation - Portal to document.body to escape stacking contexts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows bottom sheet on mobile when expanding tool - [ ] Desktop retains inline expansion behavior - [ ] Sheet slides up with spring animation - [ ] Swipe down to dismiss works - [ ] Backdrop tap closes sheet - [ ] Focus trapped inside sheet - [ ] Escape key closes - [ ] Content scrollable within sheet - [ ] GitHub link accessible in sheet","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:52:25.875281504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:22:26.460230109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:22:26.460151013Z","close_reason":"Integrated mobile bottom sheet into TldrToolGrid. On mobile ( = {}; for (const { id, repo } of TOOL_REPOS) { const [owner, name] = repo.split('/'); const { data } = await octokit.repos.get({ owner, repo: name }); stars[id] = data.stargazers_count; console.log(`[STARS] ${id}: ${data.stargazers_count}`); } return stars; } // Write to lib/data/tldr-stars.json ``` ### Update tldr-data.ts ```typescript import starsData from './data/tldr-stars.json'; export const tldrTools: TldrTool[] = [ { id: 'ntm', stars: starsData.ntm ?? 0, // ... } ]; ``` ### Build script integration ```json { "scripts": { "prebuild": "bun run scripts/fetch-tool-stars.ts", "build": "next build" } } ``` ## Fallback Handling ```typescript // If stars data is missing or stale, use hardcoded fallbacks const DEFAULT_STARS: Record = { 'ntm': 69, 'agent-mail': 1400, // ... from content.ts }; export const getToolStars = (toolId: string): number => { return starsData[toolId] ?? DEFAULT_STARS[toolId] ?? 0; }; ``` ## Caching Strategy - Stars cached in JSON file - Updated on each build - Fallback to hardcoded values if fetch fails - No runtime API calls ## Error Handling ```typescript async function fetchStarsSafely() { try { return await fetchAllStars(); } catch (error) { console.warn('[STARS] Failed to fetch, using cached values'); return existingStarsData; } } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 13 tools have star counts - [ ] Stars update on each deploy - [ ] Build doesn't fail if GitHub API unavailable - [ ] Fallback values reasonable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:28:10.687009880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.646674015Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.646576424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5s9","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-djw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5si","title":"Add animated data flow between flywheel nodes","description":"# Task: Animated Data Flow ## Objective Add visible animation showing how data/messages flow between tools in the flywheel. ## Current Behavior - Static lines connect tools - Hover shows connection exists ## Enhanced Behavior - Lines have subtle, continuous animation suggesting activity - On hover, the lines TO that tool pulse more prominently - Direction of flow is visible (arrows or particle direction) ## Animation Options ### Option A: Pulsing Lines - Lines have a gradient that slowly moves along the path - Simple, low-performance-cost - Shows directionality through gradient direction ### Option B: Particle Flow - Small dots travel along connection lines - More visually striking but higher perf cost - Clear directionality ### Option C: Glow Pulse - Connections glow briefly in sequence - Shows the "ripple" of data through the system - Medium complexity ## Recommended: Option A (Pulsing Gradient) - Best balance of visual impact and performance - Works well with prefers-reduced-motion (can be static) - Enhances existing design without overwhelming ## Implementation Notes - Use CSS animations or Framer Motion - Consider SVG stroke-dashoffset animation for gradient effect - Test on mobile for performance ## Files Affected - components/flywheel-visualization.tsx ## Dependencies - None (can work on existing component)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:04:47.673270271Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:23:01.668327319Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:23:01.668327319Z","close_reason":"Implemented continuous pulsing dash animation on flywheel connection lines using stroke-dasharray/dashoffset. Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion and speeds up when highlighted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} @@ -187,7 +187,7 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","title":"OpenGraph + Twitter images (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx, twitter-image.tsx)","description":"Create the two image-route files for the article, modeled on the slack articles equivalents. ## Files - `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx` - `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/twitter-image.tsx` Use Nexts `ImageResponse` (next/og) to generate 1200×630 (OG) and 1200×600 (Twitter summary-large-image) cards at build time. ## Design Match the visual identity of the slack articles OG card but rebrand for wills: - Dark background (same `#020204` base). - Title: "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday." - Subtitle: "AI agents · paid skill · attorney signs the docs." - Faint illustration echo (could reuse the parrot motif from the hero, or a cleaner document stack). - Author badge at bottom-left: "Jeffrey Emanuel · jeffreyemanuel.com". ## Dependencies - `@/assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp` (from hero-illustration bead). - Fonts loaded via the `fetch` pattern used in the existing OG files. ## Acceptance criteria - Both images generated at build time (`bun run build` produces them). - URLs render correctly when shared on Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Slack, and iMessage. - File sizes kept reasonable (<300 KB each).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:57:51.690091892Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:57:51.686933065Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.051860361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","title":"CSS styles: decide on sm- reuse vs wep- namespace (globals.css)","description":"Audit `app/globals.css` for every `sm-*` class the slack article uses (sm-scope, sm-body, sm-progress-bar, sm-drop-cap, sm-section-title, sm-subheading, sm-subheading-eyebrow, sm-code-block, sm-table-wrap, sm-table, sm-toc, sm-viz-wrap, sm-callout, sm-callout-emerald, sm-insight-card, sm-sign-off, sm-decisions, sm-display-title, sm-fade-section, sm-visible, sm-reveal-ready, etc.). ## Decision Two options: - **Reuse `sm-*`**: simplest. Zero new CSS. Works out of the box if the wills article uses the same visual language. - **Namespace to `wep-*`**: safer if we expect the wills articles visual identity to diverge (e.g., warmer palette, softer shadows). Adds ~800 lines of CSS. Default: reuse. Only introduce `wep-*` if a section bead requires a visually distinct style that cannot be expressed as a modifier class (e.g., `sm-insight-card sm-insight-card--wep-warm`). ## Action - Read `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`: list every `sm-` class referenced. - Run the article through a dry build; confirm no missing classes. - If any warm-palette variants are needed (e.g., an amber-accented callout for the "if I die tomorrow" deliverable), add them as modifier classes on the existing `sm-` definitions. ## Acceptance criteria - Article renders correctly with the existing stylesheet. - No CSS duplication unless strictly required. - Any new additions documented with a brief comment at the top of the new block.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.190879525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.186701102Z","close_reason":"Audited the wills article against the shared sm-* stylesheet surface. The article's current classes are already covered by app/globals.css, so the right decision is to reuse the existing sm-* namespace with no new wep-* block or duplicate CSS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:13.879883415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","title":"Accessibility pass: focus, alt, heading hierarchy, reduced motion","description":"Full a11y audit over the article once all sections and visualizations are in. ## Checklist - [ ] Every interactive element has a visible focus ring (Tailwind `focus-visible:ring-*`). - [ ] Heading hierarchy is correct: one `h1` (hero), then `h2` per section, `h3` per subsection. No skipped levels. - [ ] Every `` has meaningful `alt` text; decorative elements use `alt=""`. - [ ] Every visualization has a descriptive `aria-label` and, where appropriate, a `
